title: Finding Absolute Value of a Negative Integer

steps.work: Absolute value is the distance of a number from 0 on the number line. It is always non-negative.
steps.explanation: The symbol |x| means 'the absolute value of x.' Distance cannot be negative.

steps.work: -9 is 9 units to the left of 0, so |-9| = 9.
steps.explanation: We drop the negative sign because we are measuring distance, which is always positive.
Opposites always have the same absolute value because they are the same distance from 0.

steps.instruction: Which has a greater absolute value: -11 or 8?
steps.explanation: This question asks about absolute value, not which number is greater.
Remove the sign for -11 to get 11. 8 is already positive, so |8| = 8.

steps.work: 11 > 8, so -11 has a greater absolute value than 8.
steps.explanation: Even though -11 < 8 as a number, -11 is farther from zero. A number can be smaller but have a larger absolute value.
